summary: NAS100 dropped -671pts overnight to 28,977 ahead of CPI report at 08:30 ET. Short-term structure is bearish (-DI 26.7 >> +DI 15.6, ADX 33, price below 5/20-day MAs). Deep discount territory (7.8% of 28,822-30,808 range) with support cluster at 28,822-28,854 (Jun 9/10 lows). Alfred MT5 still offline — no H4/M15 data. CPI is the dominant catalyst: hot print = further downside to 28,500/27,900; soft print = sharp relief rally toward 29,400-29,700. Recommendation: NO_GO — do not position before CPI.

support: 28,822 (Jun 9 SSL sweep low — critical double-bottom level)
resistance: 29,723 (20-day MA — must reclaim for bullish flip)

ict_analysis.daily_bias: BEARISH (short-term) / MACRO BULLISH. ADX(14)=33.06, -DI(26.70) >> +DI(15.59) = STRONG DOWNTREND per mandatory trend check. Price at 28,977 is below 5-day MA (29,382) and 20-day MA (29,723). From ATH 30,808 (Jun 3), price has dropped -1,831pts (-5.9%). RSI(14)=45.58 neutral-bearish. Stochastics deeply oversold (23.09%). Price in EXTREME DISCOUNT (7.8% of 28,822-30,808 range). Jun 9 SSL sweep at 28,822 + today's low at 28,854 forming potential double-bottom support cluster. BUT: -DI dominance and below key MAs = bearish default. Macro: price still +20.5% above 50-day MA and +19% above 200-day MA.
ict_analysis.h4_context: UNABLE TO FULLY ANALYZE — Alfred MT5 offline, no H4 OHLCV data. Provisional: Dealing range 28,822-30,808 (1,986pts). Price at 28,977 = 7.8% of range (EXTREME DISCOUNT). The unmitigated bullish FVG at 29,000-29,073 (from May rally) has been BROKEN — price closed below it, which per ICT inversion rules may convert it to bearish resistance. Bearish OB zone at 30,600-30,800 (ATH distribution). Without H4 bars, cannot confirm CRT framing, specific FVG/OB mitigation, or SMT divergence. Confidence reduced -15pts for missing data.
ict_analysis.m15_status: NO VALID ENTRY — Alfred MT5 offline, no M15 OHLCV data. Cannot confirm: (1) Order Block formation, (2) Kill Zone timing, (3) CRT purge, (4) Entry confirmation. Layer 3 is COMPLETELY BLIND. Per pipeline rules: if any layer fails → NO TRADE. Additionally, CPI report at 08:30 ET is a HIGH IMPACT event that invalidates any pre-release setup.

risks.event: CPI Report (May) — HIGHEST IMPACT — released TODAY
Iran war escalation / oil price shock (ongoing geopolitical risk)
Tech sector de-rating / AI spending skepticism
Bond yield spike (10Y rate repricing on CPI)
Alfred MT5 offline — no live H4/M15 data for ICT pipeline
Double-bottom failure at 28,822 — would accelerate selling

recommendation: NO_GO — Four compounding reasons: (1) CPI report at 08:30 ET TODAY is the highest-impact macro event this month — positioning before CPI is gambling, not trading. April CPI was +0.6% MoM (hot), and another hot print could trigger a selloff toward 28,500 and the 50-day MA (27,922). A soft print could trigger a +500pt relief rally. Either way, pre-CPI positioning is a coin flip. (2) Alfred MT5 offline — cannot complete Layer 2 (H4) or Layer 3 (M15) of ICT pipeline. Without live OHLCV data, any entry is blind. (3) Short-term structure is confirmed bearish: ADX(14)=33 with -DI(26.7) >> +DI(15.6), price below 5-day and 20-day MAs, -1,831pts from ATH. (4) The bullish FVG at 29,000-29,073 has been broken — potential inversion to resistance. STAND ASIDE until: (a) CPI passes and market digests it, (b) Alfred MT5 comes back online, (c) H4/M15 structure confirms a setup. Post-CPI watch: if price holds 28,822 double-bottom and reclaims 29,073 (broken FVG), that's a bullish reversal signal. If 28,822 breaks, next support is 28,750 then 27,922 (50-day MA).
